# Science & Technology

Topics in the **Science & Technology** category of *the year 2012*.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Summary |
|-------|---------|
| [Higgs Boson Discovery](higgs-boson-discovery.md) | CERN announced the discovery of a new boson of about 125 GeV/c² on July 4, completing the Standard Model and confirming the Brout–Englert–Higgs mechanism by which fundamental particles acquire mass. |
| [Curiosity Rover Mars Landing](curiosity-rover-mars-landing.md) | NASA's Mars Science Laboratory rover landed in Gale Crater on August 6 via the "sky crane" maneuver, later confirming ancient Mars could have supported microbial life. |
| [Instagram Facebook Acquisition 2012](instagram-facebook-acquisition-2012.md) | Facebook acquired the two-year-old, 13-employee photo-sharing app Instagram on April 9 in a deal announced at about $1 billion (about $715 million at its September close), establishing the acquire-and-operate-independently playbook for platform consolidation. |
| [Windows 8 Release](windows-8-release.md) | Microsoft's October 26 release replaced the Start Menu with the full-screen, touch-first Metro interface; the divisive redesign saw the slowest adoption since Vista and triggered lasting changes to Microsoft's development practices. |
| [Apple iPhone 5 Launch](apple-iphone-5-launch.md) | Apple's September 21 flagship introduced a 4-inch display, the Lightning connector, LTE, and the custom A6 chip, selling 5 million units in its opening weekend; its launch was marred by the flawed debut of Apple Maps. |
| [2012 Arctic Sea Ice Minimum](arctic-sea-ice-minimum-2012.md) | Arctic sea ice fell to a record satellite-era low of 3.41 million km² on September 16 — 49% below the 1979–2000 average — a milestone of accelerating climate change that remained unbroken more than a decade later. |
| [Venus Transit of June 2012](venus-transit-june-2012.md) | Venus crossed the solar disk on June 5–6 in the last transit of Venus until 2117; observed from the ISS, SDO, and HINODE, its modern scientific value lay in calibrating the transit-spectroscopy techniques used to characterize exoplanet atmospheres. |
| [Felix Baumgartner Red Bull Stratos (October 2012)](red-bull-stratos-2012.md) | Felix Baumgartner's October 14 balloon-borne jump from about 39 km over New Mexico made him the first person to break the sound barrier in freefall (Mach 1.25); the mission set three FAI-certified records and its YouTube live stream drew more than 8 million concurrent viewers, a streaming record at the time. |
| [SOPA/PIPA 2012 Internet Activism](sopa-pipa-2012-activism.md) | The January 18 "Blackout Day" protest against the proposed SOPA and PIPA anti-piracy bills — Wikipedia, Reddit, and thousands of sites going dark while Google gathered millions of petition signatures — collapsed the legislation within days and became a landmark case of internet-driven political mobilization. |
| [Quantum Computing Advances 2012](quantum-computing-advances-2012.md) | The year quantum computing moved toward industrial investment: D-Wave's 512-qubit "Vesuvius" annealer and its Google/NASA partnerships, the Haroche–Wineland Nobel Prize in Physics for quantum-control techniques, advances in superconducting-qubit coherence, and John Preskill's coining of "quantum supremacy." |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
